content.problemStatement:
In this task, you will create a program for a birthday party organizer to determine whether a participant's age is even or odd.

**Requirements:**


Prompt the user to input the participant's age as an integer.
Use the **modulo operator (%)** to check if the age is even or odd:
Use an **if-else statement** to implement this logic.
Make sure to use the `.strip()` function to remove any extra spaces from the input before processing it.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000), the number of participants. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer age (0 ≤ age ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each age, output a line with either "Your age is even!" if the age is even, or "Your age is odd!" if the age is odd.

sampleTestCases.output: Your age is odd!\nYour age is even!
sampleTestCases.explanation: 23 is odd, so the program prints the odd message; 20 is even, so it prints the even message.

sampleTestCases.output: Your age is even!\nYour age is odd!\nYour age is even!
sampleTestCases.explanation: 0 and 1000000000 are even numbers, while 1 is odd.
Zero is considered even, so the program prints the even message.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput: Your age is even!\nYour age is odd!\nYour age is even!

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.expectedOutput:
Your age is even!
Your age is odd!
Your age is even!
Your age is odd!

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
Your age is odd!
Your age is odd!
Your age is even!
Your age is odd!
Your age is even!

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times reading each age. Use the modulo operator (%) to test age % 2. If the remainder is 0 the number is even, otherwise it is odd. Print the corresponding message for each test case. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of T.
